name: Déjà Vu
description: Dual fader utility with voltage source behavior, attenuator, attenuverter, crossfader, preamp gain up to 10x, cascading outputs, and CV or audio processing in 4HP.

controls.name: FADER
controls.description: Sets the channel level or crossfade position depending on mode.

controls.name: FADER MODE
controls.description: Switches between attenuverter, attenuator, and crossfader behavior.

controls.name: FADER GAIN
controls.description: Sets the gain stage for the fader path.

controls.name: Cascading output jumper
controls.description: Routes channel A into channel B when OUT A is left unpatched.

inputs.name: FADER A
inputs.description: Main fader input for channel A. Normalled to a voltage source when unpatched.

inputs.name: ADD A
inputs.description: Auxiliary input summed after the fader path on channel A (or second side of the crossfade in crossfader mode).

inputs.name: FADER B
inputs.description: Main fader input for channel B. Normalled to a voltage source when unpatched.

inputs.name: ADD B
inputs.description: Auxiliary input summed after the fader path on channel B (or second side of the crossfade in crossfader mode).

outputs.name: OUT A
outputs.description: Channel A output with green/red polarity LED indication.

outputs.name: OUT B
outputs.description: Channel B output with green/red polarity LED. Channel A is mixed in when OUT A is unpatched and the cascading jumper is enabled.

behaviors.name: Voltage source mode
behaviors.description: The fader becomes a static voltage source instead of a level control, so each channel can act as an offset or bias generator — or the fader acts as a manual level for whatever is patched into ADD.

behaviors.name: Three-way fader behavior
behaviors.description: The same channel can act as an attenuverter, attenuator, or crossfader between FADER and ADD, which keeps the panel useful for both audio and CV duties.

behaviors.name: Preamp gain
behaviors.description: The channel can boost incoming audio up to 10x with a low-noise preamp, making it usable as a line-level to eurorack-level booster as well as a utility attenuator.

behaviors.name: Cascade mixer
behaviors.trigger: leave OUT A unpatched (cascade jumper on)
behaviors.description: Channel A mixes into channel B output, effectively turning the pair into a 4-input mixer (FADER A + ADD A + FADER B + ADD B → OUT B).

behaviors.name: CV or audio utility
behaviors.trigger: patch either audio or control voltages
behaviors.description: The module processes both CV and audio cleanly, so it works as a bias box, crossfade pair, gain stage, stereo panner, or small mixer.

behaviors.name: Polarity LED indication
behaviors.description: Each output LED glows green for positive voltage and red for negative, with brightness tracking magnitude — a visual meter for attenuverter, offset and crossfade positions.

patchIdeas.name: Voltage offset pair
patchIdeas.patch: Leave both FADER inputs open · set each fader to different positions · use the two channels as offset sources for pitch or filter modulation · a tiny analog bias box with hands-on control.
steps: Set FADER MODE on both channels to attenuator.
Set FADER GAIN on both channels to 1x.
Leave FADER A and FADER B inputs unpatched (so the faders become voltage sources).
Patch an LFO → Déjà Vu ADD A (the LFO rides on top of the channel-A offset).
Patch Déjà Vu OUT A → Filter CUTOFF.
Patch Déjà Vu OUT B → VCO FINE TUNE or another CV destination (fader B is a pure manual offset).
Ride FADER A to set LFO depth/bias, ride FADER B to hand-tune the destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT A — LFO + manual bias, CV. OUT B — static manual offset, CV for any modulation destination.
patchIdeas.hear: Filter cutoff breathing around a position you set with FADER A, and a second parameter you nudge in real time by sliding FADER B. Green/red LEDs show polarity.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Déjà Vu:
* FADER A MODE: attenuator
* FADER B MODE: attenuator
* FADER GAIN: 1x

LFO:
Filter:
VCO:

- LFO (Out) >> Déjà Vu (ADD A)
- Déjà Vu (OUT A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Déjà Vu (OUT B) >> VCO (Fine Tune)

patchIdeas.name: Crossfade macro
patchIdeas.patch: Use channel A as a crossfader between two modulation sources · feed the result into channel B for fine trimming · one fader performs the macro move while the second sets the final range.
steps: Set FADER A MODE to crossfader (blends FADER A against ADD A).
Set FADER B MODE to attenuator (B trims the final depth).
Set both FADER GAIN switches to 1x.
Patch LFO → Déjà Vu FADER A (source 1 of the crossfade).
Patch Envelope → Déjà Vu ADD A (source 2 of the crossfade).
Patch Déjà Vu OUT A → Déjà Vu FADER B.
Patch Déjà Vu OUT B → Filter CUTOFF.
Move FADER A to morph between LFO and envelope modulation. Use FADER B to set overall depth.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT B — a single macro CV that crossfades between LFO and envelope modulation, scaled by FADER B.
patchIdeas.hear: Filter movement that morphs from continuous LFO sway to punchy per-note envelope bumps as you slide FADER A, while FADER B sets how strong the whole effect is.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Déjà Vu:
* FADER A MODE: crossfader
* FADER B MODE: attenuator
* FADER GAIN: 1x

LFO:
Envelope:
Filter:

- LFO (Out) >> Déjà Vu (FADER A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Déjà Vu (ADD A)
- Déjà Vu (OUT A) >> Déjà Vu (FADER B)
- Déjà Vu (OUT B) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo line preamp
patchIdeas.patch: Use the 10x gain setting to lift line-level or quiet synth sources into modular level · channel A and B handle left and right paths separately · useful before an effect return or mixer insert.
steps: Set FADER A MODE and FADER B MODE to attenuator.
Set both FADER GAIN switches to 10x (low-noise preamp).
Patch Line Source L → Déjà Vu FADER A.
Patch Line Source R → Déjà Vu FADER B.
Patch Déjà Vu OUT A → Output mixer L.
Patch Déjà Vu OUT B → Output mixer R.
Slide FADER A and FADER B up from zero until the green LEDs peak without flashing hard red on the loudest passages.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT A / OUT B — line-level stereo signal boosted to eurorack level, ready for a mixer or effect return.
patchIdeas.hear: A previously-too-quiet external synth or phone output sitting at modular level with clear stereo — bright green LEDs on peaks, no obvious noise floor thanks to the low-noise preamp.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Déjà Vu:
* FADER A MODE: attenuator
* FADER B MODE: attenuator
* FADER GAIN: 10x

Source L:
Source R:
Output:

- Source L (Out) -> Déjà Vu (FADER A)
- Source R (Out) -> Déjà Vu (FADER B)
- Déjà Vu (OUT A) -> Output (L)
- Déjà Vu (OUT B) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Cascade 4-input mixer
patchIdeas.patch: Patch several sources into A and B, leave OUT A unpatched, and let the cascade jumper fold both channels into a small mixer. The module turns into a compact two-stage mix block.
steps: Confirm the rear CASCADING OUTPUT JUMPER is set to ON (factory default on most units).
Set FADER A MODE and FADER B MODE to attenuator.
Set both FADER GAIN switches to 1x (raise if sources are quiet).
Patch Source 1 → Déjà Vu FADER A, Source 2 → Déjà Vu ADD A.
Patch Source 3 → Déjà Vu FADER B, Source 4 → Déjà Vu ADD B.
Leave Déjà Vu OUT A unpatched so channel A cascades into B.
Patch Déjà Vu OUT B → Output (In).
Use FADER A to balance sources 1 vs 2 (A sub-mix), FADER B to set channel B level in the final mix; ADD levels are fixed unity sums.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT B — mono sum of up to four inputs, with FADER A acting as a sub-mix level for A into B and FADER B as the channel-B level.
patchIdeas.hear: A four-into-one mix in 4HP. FADER A controls how loud the sources patched into A appear in the final mix; FADER B controls the channel-B pair. OUT B's LED shows overall level.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Déjà Vu:
* FADER A MODE: attenuator
* FADER B MODE: attenuator
* Cascading jumper: ON

Source 1:
Source 2:
Source 3:
Source 4:
Output:

- Source 1 (Out) -> Déjà Vu (FADER A)
- Source 2 (Out) -> Déjà Vu (ADD A)
- Source 3 (Out) -> Déjà Vu (FADER B)
- Source 4 (Out) -> Déjà Vu (ADD B)
- Déjà Vu (OUT B) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bias and boost
patchIdeas.patch: Bias a hot audio signal with the fader's voltage-source behavior and push it into an ADD input from another oscillator or envelope · the result is a simple offset-and-gain stage with a very direct feel.
steps: Set FADER A MODE to attenuverter (bipolar processing of FADER A).
Set FADER A GAIN to 2x to lift the signal after processing.
Patch VCO → Déjà Vu FADER A.
Patch Envelope → Déjà Vu ADD A (the envelope offsets/biases the audio path).
Patch Déjà Vu OUT A → Filter / Output.
Centre FADER A and move it up (positive) or down (inverted) to attenuvert the VCO; nudge FADER B (unpatched) only if you want its voltage to bias — not needed here.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT A — VCO attenuverted by FADER A plus the envelope added in at unity, at 2x gain.
patchIdeas.hear: A VCO that can be swept through silence and into polarity-inverted territory by FADER A, with an envelope bump from ADD A adding a per-note bias — saturating gently thanks to the 2x boost.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Déjà Vu:
* FADER A MODE: attenuverter
* FADER GAIN: 2x

VCO:
Envelope:
Output:

- VCO (Out) -> Déjà Vu (FADER A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Déjà Vu (ADD A)
- Déjà Vu (OUT A) -> Output (In)
